title: story-16-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of story-16-review-churn. In the current window, story 16 recorded another successful reviewer run at 2026-05-29T00:49:00.360663+00:00, bringing the cumulative reviewer cycle count to 13 while remaining active in-window. There are no failed runs or retry-storm signals here; the concern is structural non-convergence rather than execution failure.

The immediate impact is continued cost burn and delayed story advancement. The flagged watcher note reports cumulative dev+review cost of $5.8885 for story 16, and the latest reviewer run added another successful pass rather than resolution. This pattern is already well beyond the documented convergence ceiling of roughly 3 active review cycles.

Because the runs are succeeding, the evidence does not point to runtime breakage, provider failure, or a single explicit error message. The plausible explanation supported by the pattern is that the dev/reviewer loop is not converging on completion, with accumulated review feedback not translating into closure. Other historically high-churn stories are inactive in this window, so the active concern is specifically story 16.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Story 16 recorded another successful reviewer run and is now actively spinning at 13 cumulative review cycles, well past the normal convergence ceiling.

escalation_reason: Active review-churn on story 16 has persisted across prior concern intervals and is now at 13 reviewer cycles with $5.8885 cumulative dev+review cost. L3 judgment is needed because this is sustained non-convergence with growing impact, not a transient run failure.
